steps.title: Find the central angle
steps.content: From the figure, $P$ and $T$ are endpoints of a diameter, so angle POT is $180^\circ$. The two given $30^\circ$ angles sit at the ends of that straight angle, leaving the middle central angle.

steps.title: Subtract the known angles
steps.content:
Subtract the two known endpoint angles from the straight central angle.
$$180^\circ-30^\circ-30^\circ=120^\circ$$

steps.title: Convert degrees to radians
steps.content:
$$120^\circ\cdot\frac{\pi}{180^\circ}=\frac{2\pi}{3}$$
This is the requested radian measure. The answer is C.
